title: Instrumentality vs Buddhism
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality vs. Buddhism             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Mon Jul 26, 2010 12:45 am The other day me and my father (who is currently watching NGE) were discussing the type of Buddhism he uses to cope with his depression. The main "goal" of Buddhism, or at least my dad's version, is to achieve a state of "perfect consciousness," or when you have completely stripped back your ego and mask you wear at other people and found the true humanity within yourself, and have accepted that nothing will last forever (and that means NOTHING, which is a major disagreement topic with me.) What this does is allows people to be totally free from inhibitions and fears. Me and him had a long conversation about this, and due to me watching NGE and my own existentialist beliefs, I disagreed with this but saw the direct parallels to Eva and especially EoE. In fact, the terms "ego" and "consiousness" are used in Eva. I think that the Tanging at the end of EoE was supposed to be like if everyone reached this state of "perfection" all at once, and how this would be such a negative thing to the world. Although EoE is about Shinji accepting things, it's not about his own mortality or such, it's about accepting that happiness can always exist, but there will also be pain and suffering, and that you have to struggle through that to achieve happiness, and, of course, not run away from the pain. Maybe Anno had studied Buddhism in his depression as a way to seek help, and found the flaws in it, and portrayed them.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jul 26, 2010 1:11 am The ego used in buddhist terminology and the ego used in psychoanalytical and Evangelion terminology are nowhere near the same thing. Just FYI.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Mon Jul 26, 2010 1:17 am AuraTwilight wrote: The ego used in buddhist terminology and the ego used in psychoanalytical and Evangelion terminology are nowhere near the same thing. Just FYI.

[]: Instrumentality vs. Buddhism             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Mon Jul 26, 2010 12:45 am The other day me and my father (who is currently watching NGE) were discussing the type of Buddhism he uses to cope with his depression. What this does is allows people to be totally free from inhibitions and fears. Me and him had a long conversation about this, and due to me watching NGE and my own existentialist beliefs, I disagreed with this but saw the direct parallels to Eva and especially EoE. I think that the Tanging at the end of EoE was supposed to be like if everyone reached this state of "perfection" all at once, and how this would be such a negative thing to the world.
